description:
The README never mentions muzak/muzak_pro (the established Elixir mutation tool by Devon Estes) — zero hits repo-wide outside one internal ADR note. First question from any community reader is 'why not muzak?' and there's no answer.

Add a README section near the top that states the differentiated niche, which exists today only in internal ADRs:
- JSON-first, single-document output (no pretty human output by design) — .spec/decisions/no_pretty_output_v1.md
- Content-addressed, stable mutation IDs across `mix format` — content_addressed_ids.md
- Deterministic / serial-by-default for reproducibility — serial_execution_and_seed.md
- Primary consumer is an LLM verifier/judge, not a human — the real thesis
- In-process pipeline (vs muzak's child-BEAM) and why — in_process_pipeline.md

Acceptance: a reader understands why this exists and how it differs from muzak within the first screen of the README.

Tests added: none (docs-only change; no README-content test harness exists in this repo)
Verification stubs flipped: none (ticket advances no spec-verification stub; it is a README/positioning task with no .spec subject contract)
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Added a "## Why mutagen_ex (and how it differs from muzak)" section to README.md, placed immediately after the opening description and before "## Install" so it lands within the first screen (acceptance criterion). The section names muzak/muzak_pro (Devon Estes) explicitly and answers "why not muzak?", then states the differentiated niche surfaced from internal ADRs: JSON-first single-document output (no_pretty_output_v1.md), content-addressed stable mutation IDs across mix format (content_addressed_ids.md), deterministic serial-by-default execution (serial_execution_and_seed.md), the LLM-verifier/judge as the primary consumer (the real thesis tying the others together), and the in-process pipeline vs muzak's child-BEAM with its honest trade-offs (in_process_pipeline.md, self_mutation_refused.md). All five ADR links resolve. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test --seed 0 green (640 tests, 0 failures); the diff touches no code.

title: README positioning: why mutagen_ex (vs muzak) + LLM-oracle thesis
